method_summary: The authors built a high-throughput automated executor to implement and evaluate LLM-generated research ideas in parallel, using the execution results as feedback to guide an evolutionary search and reinforcement learning process for the ideator model.
key_innovation: A large-scale, high-throughput automated idea executor capable of implementing and evaluating hundreds of LLM-generated research ideas for GPU-intensive, open-ended research problems in parallel.
best_result: Execution-guided evolutionary search found a post-training recipe outperforming the GRPO baseline by 21.4 percentage points on math reasoning.
limitations_stated: RL causes the ideator model to converge on a few easy-to-implement ideas, resulting in a collapse in thinking length and idea diversity.
Only Claude-4.5-Opus shows a clear scaling curve, while both Claude-4.5-Sonnet and GPT-5 tend to saturate early.
compute_required: GPU-intensive (implied by LLM pre-training and post-training on 1.5B models)
title: Towards Execution-Grounded Automated AI Research
